  AND s.date <= (SELECT max(date) FROM sales_daily)

LIMIT 5

fixed.sql:
WITH today AS (
  SELECT max(date) AS dt FROM sales_daily
),
top5 AS (
  SELECT sku_id, sum(yhat) AS forecast_units
  FROM forecast_daily, today
  WHERE model_name = 'lightgbm'
    AND date > today.dt
    AND date <= today.dt + INTERVAL 28 DAY
  GROUP BY sku_id
  ORDER BY forecast_units DESC
  LIMIT 5
)
SELECT t.sku_id,
       p.product_name,
       t.forecast_units,
       coalesce(a.actual_28d, 0) AS actual_28d
FROM top5 t
JOIN products p USING (sku_id)
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT sku_id, sum(units_sold) AS actual_28d
  FROM sales_daily, today
  WHERE date > today.dt - INTERVAL 28 DAY
    AND date <= today.dt
  GROUP BY sku_id
) a USING (sku_id)
ORDER BY t.forecast_units DESC

rag.sql:
WITH today AS (SELECT max(date) AS dt FROM sales_daily),
forecast_tot AS (
  SELECT sku_id, sum(yhat) AS forecast_total
  FROM forecast_daily, today
  WHERE model_name = 'lightgbm' AND date > today.dt AND date <= today.dt + INTERVAL 28 DAY
  GROUP BY sku_id
),
actual_tot AS (
  SELECT sku_id, sum(units_sold) AS actual_total
  FROM sales_daily, today
  WHERE date > today.dt - INTERVAL 28 DAY AND date <= today.dt
  GROUP BY sku_id
)
SELECT f.sku_id, p.product_name, f.forecast_total, COALESCE(a.actual_total, 0) AS actual_total
FROM forecast_tot f
JOIN products p ON f.sku_id = p.sku_id
LEFT JOIN actual_tot a ON f.sku_id = a.sku_id
ORDER BY f.forecast_total DESC, f.sku_id
LIMIT 5

SELECT model_name, avg_mape, avg_wrmsse
FROM model_avg

fixed.sql:
WITH single_source AS (
  SELECT material_pn
  FROM supply_split
  GROUP BY material_pn
  HAVING count(*) = 1
),
red_risk AS (
  SELECT material_pn
  FROM material_risk
  WHERE eval_date = (SELECT max(eval_date) FROM material_risk)
    AND risk_level = 'RED'
)
SELECT r.material_pn, m.material_name, b.sku_id, p.product_name
FROM red_risk r
JOIN single_source s ON r.material_pn = s.material_pn
JOIN bom b ON r.material_pn = b.material_pn
JOIN products p ON b.sku_id = p.sku_id
JOIN materials m ON r.material_pn = m.material_pn
ORDER BY r.material_pn, b.sku_id

rag.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT p.product_name
FROM material_risk r
JOIN bom b ON r.material_pn = b.material_pn
JOIN products p ON b.sku_id = p.sku_id
WHERE r.eval_date = (SELECT max(eval_date) FROM material_risk)
  AND r.risk_level = 'RED'
  AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM supply_split s WHERE s.material_pn = r.material_pn) = 1
ORDER BY p.product_name

fixed.sql:
WITH max_shared AS (
  SELECT material_pn, count(DISTINCT sku_id) AS sku_count
  FROM bom
  GROUP BY material_pn
  HAVING count(DISTINCT sku_id) = (
    SELECT max(c) FROM (SELECT count(DISTINCT sku_id) AS c FROM bom GROUP BY material_pn) t
  )
)
SELECT ms.material_pn, m.material_name, ms.sku_count, NULL AS sku_id, NULL AS product_name
FROM max_shared ms
JOIN materials m USING (material_pn)
UNION ALL
SELECT ms.material_pn, m.material_name, NULL, b.sku_id, p.product_name
FROM max_shared ms
JOIN materials m ON ms.material_pn = m.material_pn
JOIN bom b ON ms.material_pn = b.material_pn
JOIN products p ON b.sku_id = p.sku_id
ORDER BY material_pn, sku_id NULLS FIRST

rag.sql:
WITH bom_counts AS (
    SELECT material_pn, COUNT(DISTINCT sku_id) AS sku_count
    FROM bom
    GROUP BY material_pn
),
max_count AS (
    SELECT MAX(sku_count) AS max_c FROM bom_counts
)
SELECT b.material_pn, b.sku_id
FROM bom b
JOIN bom_counts bc ON b.material_pn = bc.material_pn
JOIN max_count m ON bc.sku_count = m.max_c
ORDER BY b.material_pn, b.sku_id

  (SELECT sum(yhat) FROM forecast_daily WHERE model_name = 'lightgbm') AS lightgbm_forecast_28d,
  (SELECT sum(units_sold)
     FROM sales_daily
     WHERE date BETWEEN (SELECT max(date) FROM sales_daily) - INTERVAL 27 DAY
                    AND (SELECT max(date) FROM sales_daily)
  ) AS actual_sales_28d

rag.sql:
SELECT
  (SELECT COALESCE(SUM(yhat), 0) FROM forecast_daily
   WHERE model_name = 'lightgbm'
     AND date > (SELECT MAX(date) FROM sales_daily)
     AND date <= (SELECT MAX(date) FROM sales_daily) + INTERVAL 28 DAY) AS forecast_28d_total,
  (SELECT COALESCE(SUM(units_sold), 0) FROM sales_daily
   WHERE date > (SELECT MAX(date) FROM sales_daily) - INTERVAL 28 DAY) AS actual_28d_total

    FROM open_po o
    WHERE o.material_pn = r.material_pn
      AND o.eta_date > (SELECT max(date) FROM sales_daily)

rag.sql: SELECT risk_level, COUNT(*) AS material_count FROM material_risk WHERE eval_date = (SELECT max(eval_date) FROM material_risk) GROUP BY risk_level ORDER BY risk_level
